# UX and Testability Checklist: Crosslog Activity Rail Redesign

**Purpose**: Validate that redesign, accessibility, Figma traceability, and cross-OS UI/E2E requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all mandatory Activity Rail shell regions documented as requirements, including topbar, activity rail, pane workspace, per-pane headers, popovers, log viewport, and status bar? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Contract figma-design-contract §Mandatory MVP Regions]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for every visible MVP control in the Figma frame and for every visible future control that must remain unavailable in the MVP? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011-FR-015, Contract figma-design-contract §Scope Boundaries]
- [x] CHK003 Are pane header requirements complete for file-backed panes, directory-backed panes, active panes, live panes, deleted sources, offset display, search entry, and long titles? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004-FR-005, §FR-026-FR-028, §FR-058, §FR-063-FR-065]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for both empty and non-empty workspace states in the redesigned shell? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008, §FR-010, Contract figma-design-contract §Acceptance Contract]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for browser capability limitations without implying Desktop-only monitoring or automatic discovery support? [Completeness, Spec §FR-081-FR-082, User Story 7]
- [x] CHK006 Are requirements defined for all required UI/E2E surfaces across Web, Desktop WDIO, and macOS XCTest/Accessibility coverage? [Coverage, Spec §CR-006, §SC-013, Contract test-automation §Required UI/E2E Coverage]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is the relationship between the Figma node `11:3` and the written requirements specific enough to decide which visual elements are mandatory, optional, or out of scope? [Clarity, Spec Design Reference, Contract figma-design-contract §Source]
- [x] CHK008 Is "consistent with the reference design" clarified with concrete layout regions, control placement, state visibility, and scope boundaries? [Ambiguity, Spec Design Reference, Spec §FR-010, Contract figma-design-contract §Acceptance Contract]
- [x] CHK009 Are command entry requirements specific about available MVP actions and excluded non-MVP actions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012-FR-013]
- [x] CHK010 Are future Activity Rail control requirements clear about unavailable states, user-facing communication, and prohibited behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, §FR-083-FR-084, Contract ui-behavior §Future Feature Guardrails]
- [x] CHK011 Are "visually distinguishable", "live status", and "status indicators" clarified with enough non-implementation criteria to support objective review? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-027, §FR-065, Contract ui-behavior §Live, Deleted, and Error States]
- [x] CHK012 Are long-label and no-overlap requirements specific about affected labels, primary controls, supported viewport classes, and acceptable truncation behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-028, §FR-087, §SC-014]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 Are Activity Rail search requirements consistent between workspace-level entry points and per-pane search independence? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, §FR-037-FR-041, User Story 3]
- [x] CHK014 Are synchronization requirements consistent between topbar control, active pane semantics, status bar summary, and per-pane time offsets? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016, §FR-050-FR-062, User Story 2]
- [x] CHK015 Are directory navigation requirements consistent between pane header requirements, file ordering rules, empty-directory behavior, and replacement semantics? [Consistency, Spec §FR-029-FR-036, §FR-085, User Story 4]
- [x] CHK016 Are session restore requirements consistent about what is restored, what is intentionally excluded, and how the redesigned shell participates in restoration? [Consistency, Spec §FR-069-FR-071, §CR-004, User Story 7]
- [x] CHK017 Are platform parity requirements consistent with documented platform constraints for browser, Windows, macOS, and Linux targets? [Consistency, Spec §FR-080-FR-082, §FR-086, §CR-003]
- [x] CHK018 Are test automation requirements consistent between the specification success criteria, implementation plan gates, and test automation contract? [Consistency, Spec §SC-013, Plan §Testing, Contract test-automation §GitHub Actions Gate]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Can each user story independent test be traced to functional requirements and measurable success criteria without relying on visual preference alone? [Traceability, Spec User Stories, Spec §FR-001-FR-087, Spec §SC-001-SC-014]
- [x] CHK020 Are usability success criteria quantified with target population, task conditions, timing method, and pass/fail thresholds? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002-SC-004]
- [x] CHK021 Are performance success criteria tied to explicit reference test conditions that are documented before implementation work begins? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005-SC-007, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK022 Are synchronization accuracy criteria specific about sampling strategy, timestamp fixtures, and expected tolerance for overlapping timestamps? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008, Spec §FR-053]
- [x] CHK023 Are live-update and reliability criteria specific about event count, platform applicability, and source failure classes? [Measurability, Spec §SC-009, §SC-011, Spec §FR-063-FR-065, §FR-078-FR-079]
- [x] CHK024 Are UI/E2E acceptance criteria complete enough to determine when every MVP user scenario is covered on every supported target where applicable? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-013, Contract test-automation §Required UI/E2E Coverage]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Are primary, alternate, exception, recovery, and non-functional scenario classes represented across the seven user stories? [Coverage, Spec User Stories, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK026 Are alternate flows defined for topbar actions, activity rail actions, command entry actions, pane header actions, and popover entry points? [Gap, Spec §FR-011-FR-015, §FR-026, §FR-040, §FR-059]
- [x] CHK027 Are recovery requirements defined for unexpected application errors, stale session state, missing files, replaced files, unreadable files, and browser capability limits? [Coverage, Spec §FR-063-FR-064, §FR-069-FR-071, §FR-076-FR-079, §FR-082]
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements defined for preserving analysis context when multiple panes are affected by independent source, search, synchronization, or encoding states? [Coverage, Spec §FR-037-FR-042, §FR-054-FR-055, §FR-063, §FR-079]
- [x] CHK029 Are requirements defined for future extension slots without creating MVP obligations or hidden dependencies on unplanned features? [Coverage, Spec §FR-015, §FR-083-FR-084, Contract ui-behavior §Future Feature Guardrails]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK030 Are all listed visual edge cases mapped to explicit requirements or acceptance criteria, especially excessive pane count, long labels, unavailable commands, and unsupported platform capabilities? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec §FR-024-FR-028, §FR-082, §FR-087]
- [x] CHK031 Are invalid-input requirements complete for regular expressions, time offsets, timestamp candidates, encoding detection, and file-size or memory limits? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-042, §FR-048, §FR-061, §FR-073-FR-077]
- [x] CHK032 Are empty and boundary-state requirements documented for empty directories, directory navigation endpoints, panes without timestamps, and no-open-source workspace state? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008, §FR-032, §FR-054, §FR-085]
- [x] CHK033 Are requirements defined for log content that resembles commands, links, terminal escapes, or executable instructions without weakening inert rendering and read-only constraints? [Security, Spec Edge Cases, Spec §CR-001-CR-002]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK034 Are accessibility requirements specified for keyboard access, accessible names, focus movement, icon-only controls, popovers, disabled future controls, and status communication? [Accessibility, Spec §FR-012, §FR-026, §FR-040, §FR-059, Contract figma-design-contract §Acceptance Contract]
- [x] CHK035 Are viewport and responsive requirements specified with supported dimensions, no-overlap expectations, and essential-control preservation criteria? [Accessibility, Spec §FR-028, §FR-087, §SC-014]
- [x] CHK036 Are security and safety requirements complete for command fields, search fields, popovers, log rows, copied text, and rendered log content? [Security, Spec §FR-066-FR-068, §CR-001-CR-002, Plan §Read-Only/Security Model]
- [x] CHK037 Are CI/CD requirements measurable for local macOS gates and remote Windows/macOS/Linux automated plus UI/E2E gates? [Testability, Spec §CR-006, Plan §OS UI Test Scripts, Contract test-automation §Local Current-OS Gate]
- [x] CHK038 Are selector stability requirements documented with criteria for accessible roles, accessible names, and data-testid usage where semantics are insufficient? [Testability, Contract test-automation §Selector Stability, Tasks T004-T008]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK039 Are assumptions about Figma authority, MVP functional baseline, browser limitations, top-level directory scanning, and non-restored scroll positions documented and aligned with requirements? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK040 Are dependency decisions about the current React/Tauri/shared stack, icon strategy, UI kit rejection, and unchanged platform ports traceable to requirements rather than implementation preference alone? [Dependency, Plan §Research Conclusions, Plan §Dependency Policy]
- [x] CHK041 Are cross-OS test dependencies and runner responsibilities documented well enough to prevent local-only validation from being mistaken for release readiness? [Dependency, Plan §Phase 2.5, Contract test-automation §GitHub Actions Gate]
- [x] CHK042 Are existing-test reuse assumptions documented with boundaries for when selector updates are acceptable and when replacement UI tests are required? [Assumption, Plan §Phase 2.5, Contract test-automation §Test Reuse Rules]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK043 Is any ambiguity remaining around whether the command entry point is a palette, search field, menu, or generic action launcher for MVP purposes? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011-FR-013]
- [x] CHK044 Is any conflict remaining between showing future Activity Rail controls in the Figma layout and the MVP requirement to exclude the corresponding capabilities? [Conflict, Spec §FR-015, §FR-083-FR-084]
- [x] CHK045 Is any ambiguity remaining around how macOS XCTest/Accessibility coverage maps to Web and WDIO UI/E2E scenarios? [Ambiguity, Spec §CR-006, Contract test-automation §Required UI/E2E Coverage]
- [x] CHK046 Is any conflict remaining between platform-specific window-control adaptation and the requirement for consistent topbar, activity rail, pane workspace, and status bar structure? [Conflict, Spec §FR-086]

## Implementation Readiness After Plan Updates

- [x] CHK047 Are corrected US3-US7 UI/E2E coverage entries aligned with the user story names, acceptance scenarios, and task story labels? [Consistency, Spec User Stories 3-7, Contract test-automation §Required UI/E2E Coverage, Tasks T049-T111]
- [x] CHK048 Are the implementation-readiness tasks sequential, unambiguous, and traceable after adding the usability and performance remediation tasks? [Traceability, Tasks T112-T124]
- [x] CHK049 Are performance reference conditions documented with fixture source, build mode, debugger exclusion, warm-up runs, measured runs, and worst-run reporting rules? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005-SC-007, Plan §Performance Reference Conditions]
- [x] CHK050 Are performance success criteria traceable to both methodology documentation and an executable local performance gate? [Traceability, Spec §SC-005-SC-009, Quickstart §Performance Reference Gate, Tasks T121-T122]
- [x] CHK051 Is the SC-002 usability walkthrough requirement supported by documented protocol requirements for participant role, task setup, timing method, pass/fail criteria, and result storage? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Quickstart §Usability Walkthrough Gate, Tasks T114]
- [x] CHK052 Are macOS UI gate requirements consistent across the plan, quickstart, test automation contract, and task list after adding explicit `test:ui:desktop` wiring? [Consistency, Plan §Phase 2.5, Quickstart §Local macOS Workflow, Contract test-automation §Local Current-OS Gate, Tasks T016]
- [x] CHK053 Are GitHub Actions release gate requirements complete for Windows, macOS, and Linux automated tests, UI/E2E tests, and builds? [Coverage, Contract test-automation §GitHub Actions Gate, Plan §Phase 2.5, Tasks T017, T124]
- [x] CHK054 Are local-current-OS responsibilities and remote CI responsibilities separated clearly enough to start implementation without mistaking local macOS validation for release readiness? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §GitHub CI/CD Workflow]
- [x] CHK055 Are all prior PASS WITH ACTION items represented by concrete foundational or polish tasks before they can affect phase completion or release readiness? [Completeness, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §Post-Design Constitution Check, Tasks T016-T017, T121-T124]
- [x] CHK056 Are pre-code readiness artifacts for Figma audit, test reuse audit, and icon strategy explicitly required before UI implementation tasks begin? [Completeness, Plan §Phase 2.1, Tasks T001-T003]
- [x] CHK057 Are non-MVP Activity Rail guardrails still consistent after the UI/E2E coverage mapping correction? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, §FR-083-FR-084, Contract ui-behavior §Future Feature Guardrails, Tasks T020, T028]
- [x] CHK058 Are existing checklist items affected by the plan and task updates refined by the new readiness items without creating contradictory acceptance expectations? [Consistency, Checklist CHK019-CHK024, CHK037-CHK046]
- [x] CHK059 Are deterministic performance fixture assumptions documented enough to avoid implementation-specific benchmark interpretation during task execution? [Assumption, Plan §Performance Reference Conditions, Quickstart §Performance Reference Gate]
- [x] CHK060 Are implementation-start boundaries clear that Phase 1 and Phase 2 unblock story work, while usability walkthrough, final performance recording, build, and CI verification remain release-readiness gates? [Clarity, Tasks §Phase Dependencies, Tasks §Implementation Strategy]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist validates requirement quality only; it is not a UI test plan or implementation verification checklist.
